metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4052
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3D: Cerebellar neurons were double-stained with antibodies for NPY5R and vesicular glutamate transporter (vGlut) or vesicular GABA transporter (VGAT). NPY5R immunoreaction colocalised VGAT indicating and did not colocalise with Vglut indicating that it is part of the presynapse in inhibitory neurons of the cerebellum.
Figure 3E: Histogram quantifies colocalisation of NPY5R with vGAT and vGlut presynaptic markers.

Note on the antibody used: The paper comments on the specificity and cross-reactivity of NPY5R antibody used in the study.
Figure 3B and 3C2: The specificity of an NPY5R antibody on INS-1 cells was tested by transiently cotransfecting GFP and NPY1R, NPY2R, or NPY5R cDNAs. The NPY5R antibody specifically stained for NPY5R.
Figure 3, D3: No staining was observed when the primary or secondary antibodies were omitted.. Experimental description: Cerebellar slices were prepared from 3-wk-old C57BL/6J mice as previously described (Liu and Cull-Candy 2000). Immunostaining of cerebellar cultured neurons was performed largely as described previously (Ramamoorthy et al. 2011)
